source_anchor.source_text: Severity Level Definitions

entities.status_update_cadence: 60 minutes for Level 3; 15 minutes for Level 4

entities.tmc_action: Log SAFE status and close outreach for this traveler

entities.description: Direct Travel's binding obligation to conduct welfare checks via configured channels in priority order (SMS, email, mobile application push notification, voice call), log all attempts with timestamp a

entities.name: Client
entities.description: The contracting organization that receives incident escalation notifications and may request changes to incident severity classification.

source_anchor.source_text: Client may request a change to classification at any time; Direct Travel will consider such requests promptly and document the outcome.

relationships.description: Incidents are classified into one of four severity levels (Level 1 Advisory through Level 4 Crisis) based on direct impact to Travelers and disruption scope.
Incidents have direct and varying impacts on Travelers depending on severity level, from limited impact (Level 1) to immediate life safety risk (Level 4).
Direct Travel uses the Risk Intelligence Platform to inform commercially reasonable severity determinations and incident classification decisions.
Direct Travel engages with Client for severity classification review, escalation notifications at different severity levels, and status updates during incidents.
Traveler can confirm SAFE status via standardized response framework, indicating no immediate danger and no required assistance.
Traveler Outreach Standards obligation is assigned to Direct Travel, requiring welfare checks via configured channels in priority order, response logging, and live contact for assistance requests.
Crisis Bridge real-time conference channel is activated when Level 4 Crisis severity is determined, requiring Client escalation roster contact within 60 minutes.

entities.name: Specialist Provider
entities.description: Third-party providers of security, medical, evacuation, or other specialized risk management services who may participate in Crisis Bridges and assist with on-ground locate efforts.

source_anchor.source_text: where applicable, Specialist Provider representatives

relationships.description: The Primary Travel Program Owner contact role is designated by the Client as part of the required 24/7 escalation roster.
The obligation to maintain a current 24/7 escalation roster and notify Direct Travel of changes within five business days is assigned to the Client.
The escalation roster maintenance requirements and notification procedures are defined in the SOW.
Incidents are classified according to the Level 1 through Level 4 severity classification system, which determines escalation procedures and contact responsibilities.
Incidents are escalated to the Primary Travel Program Owner, who serves as the first point of contact for all incidents regardless of severity.
Direct Travel engages Specialist Providers for on-ground locate assistance in support of non-responsive traveler escalation efforts.
Direct Travel provides the Crisis Bridge service, establishing real-time communication channels during Level 4 incidents.
Incidents impact Travelers; escalation and communication procedures are triggered when incidents affect traveler welfare and safety.
Direct Travel is a party to the Statement of Work governing the provision of duty of care services.
The Client is a party to the Statement of Work that defines escalation roster requirements and service configurations.
